guardrail: static projection of the public register; no facts added here

data.name: Singapore -> Hong Kong SAR (SGD->HKD settlement)

divergence.reason: Directed-edge operative interaction sets (Corridor Atlas v0.2.5 §5, read at the destination inbound gate for THIS direction) differ from the undirected pairwise sets (Architecture §5.14). The Atlas generalizes the §5.12/§5.14 undirected pairs into directed edges and re-derives the operative interaction sets per direction; the compatibility CATEGORY agrees, the interaction-set list may differ by direction. This divergence is declared, not silent.

infrastructure_overlap.note: HKMA Project Ensemble and MAS Project Guardian alignment (SG and HK); SG's formal Project Ensemble membership is a Tier-2 attribution pending primary verification (verification_backlog VB-02).

inbound_mechanism.detail: Licence required to issue in Hong Kong or to issue an HKD-referenced token; the first cohort is HKD-only and bank-led, so a non-HKD or crypto-native issuer needs a later licence or partnership.

origin_override.basis: MAS Digital Token Service Provider statement (June 2025): unlicensed Singapore-based operations serving offshore customers are constrained, so the origin co-governs an otherwise destination-gated edge.
origin_override.detail: The June 2025 MAS Digital Token Service Provider statement constrains unlicensed Singapore-based operations serving offshore customers, so the origin co-governs an otherwise destination-gated edge.

data.note: The most institutionally supported corridor in the Atlas: MAS and HKMA have a banking-supervision MoU and align Project Guardian with Project Ensemble, so the tokenized-asset and digital-financial archetypes are unusually live. Even so, a MAS-regulated single-currency stablecoin must be issued in Singapore and an HKD product still requires an HKMA licence, so dual authorization remains the baseline despite the cooperation.

source.primary: Fan, Cross-Border Digital-Finance Corridor Atlas v0.2.5 (June 2026), §5.5 (Singapore -> Hong Kong SAR)
source.secondary: Fan, Cross-Border Stablecoin Architecture v3 (Twelve Jurisdictions, June 2026), §5.14 (SG x HK: Category I/II hybrid — undirected §5.14 pair; see this edge's `divergence` field)
